# Design Document ## Overview This design document outlines the technical approach for reorganizing the OmniDatum repository documentation to establish a maintainable multi-format knowledge base. The solution introduces a canonical data structure, synchronization mechanisms, and enhanced metadata tracking while preserving the existing programmatic generation workflow using the [`stargazer`](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer) tool. ### Design Principles 1. **Single Source of Truth**: All repository metadata derives from one canonical data source 2. **Format Independence**: Each output format (LIST, TABLE, README) generated from shared data 3. **Manual Override Support**: Allow manual additions and annotations that persist across regenerations 4. **Backward Compatibility**: Maintain existing file formats and link structures 5. **Extensibility**: Design for future platforms and metadata fields 6. **Validation-First**: Build verification into the generation process ## Architecture ### High-Level Architecture ```mermaid graph TB GH[GitHub API
Starred Repos] -->|fetch| SG[Stargazer Tool
encodedfox/stargazer] SG -->|generate| RAW[Raw Data
JSON] RAW -->|merge| MAN[Manual Additions
YAML] MAN -->|validate| VAL[Validation Engine] VAL -->|augment| ENH[Enhanced Metadata
Canonical Data] ENH -->|template| LIST[LIST.md] ENH -->|template| TABLE[TABLE.md] ENH -->|extract| META[Metadata Index
JSON] META -->|generate| NAV[Navigation Links] NAV -->|update| README[README.md] style ENH fill:#e1f5ff style VAL fill:#fff3e1 style NAV fill:#e8f5e9 ``` ### Data Flow 1. **Ingestion Phase**: Fetch starred repositories via GitHub API using [`encodedfox/stargazer`](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer) 2. **Augmentation Phase**: Merge with manual additions and enrich with platform tracking 3. **Validation Phase**: Check for consistency, duplicates, broken links, and data quality 4. **Generation Phase**: Produce LIST.md, TABLE.md, and metadata index 5. **Integration Phase**: Update README.md with cross-references and navigation 6. **Reporting Phase**: Generate synchronization report and quality metrics ## Components and Interfaces ### Component 1: Canonical Data Structure **Purpose**: Represent all repository and documentation metadata in a single, extensible format. **Data Schema** (extended from stargazer output): ```yaml repositories: - id: "github-com-encodedfox-stargazer" # Unique identifier platforms: - platform: "github" url: "https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer" status: "active" # active, archived, deprecated last_verified: "2024-12-10" - platform: "codeberg" url: "https://codeberg.org/example/repo" status: "active" migration_date: "2024-01-15" metadata: name: "stargazer" owner: "encodedfox" description: "Generate awesome list from your GitHub stars" primary_language: "Go" license: "MIT License" license_spdx: "MIT" stars: 150 topics: ["cli", "github", "awesome-list"] homepage: "" classification: categories: ["tools", "github-management"] readme_sections: ["GitHub Projects"] web_reference_topics: [] quality_metrics: archive_status: false last_commit_date: "2024-11-15" last_star_update: "2024-12-10" quality_score: 85 # 0-100 based on stars, activity, relevance source: "github_stars" # github_stars, manual, derived manual_projects: - id: "manual-nocobase" name: "NocoBase" description: "No-Code/Low-Code Development Platform" platforms: - platform: "github" url: "https://github.com/nocobase/nocobase" status: "active" is_primary: true metadata: primary_language: "TypeScript" license: "Apache License 2.0" classification: categories: ["low-code", "no-code", "platform"] readme_sections: ["GitHub Projects"] source: "manual" curator_notes: "Self-hosted alternative to Airtable/AppSmith" web_references: - title: "Martin Fowler's P of EAA" url: "https://www.martinfowler.com/eaaCatalog/index.html" category: "Architecture" subcategory: "Patterns" content_type: "catalog" difficulty: "intermediate" last_verified: "2024-12-10" related_repos: ["gin-gonic/gin", "go-kit/kit"] books: - title: "Designing Software Architectures" subtitle: "General + ADD" category: "Architecture" related_books: - "Software Architecture in Practice" - "Documenting Software Architectures" expansion_topics: ["distributed-systems", "data-engineering"] ``` **Interface Specification**: - Input: GitHub API JSON (from stargazer) + Manual YAML files - Output: Enhanced JSON with full metadata - Validation: JSON Schema for structure validation ### Component 2: Validation Engine **Purpose**: Ensure data quality, consistency, and completeness across all sources. **Validation Rules**: ```typescript interface ValidationRule { name: string; severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info'; check: (data: CanonicalData) => ValidationResult[]; } // Example rules: const rules: ValidationRule[] = [ { name: "no_duplicate_repos", severity: "error", check: (data) => { // Check for duplicate repo URLs across platforms } }, { name: "consistent_archive_status", severity: "error", check: (data) => { // Verify archive status matches description text } }, { name: "valid_urls", severity: "error", check: (data) => { // Verify all URLs are well-formed } }, { name: "readme_cross_reference", severity: "warning", check: (data) => { // Check if README projects exist in starred repos } }, { name: "platform_migration_complete", severity: "warning", check: (data) => { // Verify Codeberg migrations have both URLs } }, { name: "stale_content", severity: "info", check: (data) => { // Flag content >2 years old } } ]; ``` **Validation Report Format**: ```json { "timestamp": "2024-12-10T18:00:00Z", "summary": { "errors": 0, "warnings": 5, "info": 23, "total_repos": 839 }, "issues": [ { "severity": "warning", "rule": "readme_cross_reference", "message": "GitHub Projects entry 'NocoBase' not found in starred repositories", "location": "README.md#github-projects", "suggestion": "Add URL or mark as manual addition" } ], "metrics": { "repos_by_platform": { "github": 835, "codeberg": 4 }, "archived_count": 42, "missing_licenses": 87, "stale_references": 15 } } ``` ### Component 3: Template Generation Engine **Purpose**: Generate [`LIST.md`](../../LIST.md), [`TABLE.md`](../../TABLE.md), and navigation elements from canonical data. **Template System**: ```go type TemplateEngine interface { GenerateListMarkdown(data CanonicalData) (string, error) GenerateTableMarkdown(data CanonicalData) (string, error) GenerateNavigationLinks(data CanonicalData) (NavigationData, error) GenerateMetadataIndex(data CanonicalData) (MetadataIndex, error) } type RepositoryEntry struct { Name string URL string Description string License string Stars int IsArchived bool Platforms []Platform } type NavigationData struct { LanguageTOC []TOCEntry CrossReferences map[string][]string // section -> related repos ReadmeProjects []Project } ``` **LIST.md Template Pattern**: ```markdown - [owner/repo](url) - description \[*License*\] (⭐️stars) *Archived!* ``` **TABLE.md Template Pattern**: ```markdown | [owner/repo](url) | description (*archived*) | License | ⭐️stars | ``` **Platform Notation**: ```markdown - [owner/repo](github-url) - description [🔄→Codeberg](codeberg-url) \[*License*\] (⭐️stars) *Archived!* ``` ### Component 4: Cross-Reference Manager **Purpose**: Create bidirectional links between README sections and starred repository lists. **Cross-Reference Types**: 1. **README → Starred Lists**: Link from topic/concept to relevant repositories 2. **Starred Lists → README**: Inline tags showing which README sections reference the repo 3. **Web References → Repositories**: Connect patterns/concepts to implementations 4. **Books → Web References**: Link learning materials to practical resources **Implementation**: ```typescript interface CrossReference { source: { document: 'README' | 'LIST' | 'TABLE'; section: string; anchor?: string; }; target: { document: 'README' | 'LIST' | 'TABLE'; section: string; anchor?: string; }; relationship: 'implements' | 'references' | 'alternative' | 'prerequisite'; bidirectional: boolean; } // Example cross-references: const references: CrossReference[] = [ { source: { document: 'README', section: 'Databases/TiDB' }, target: { document: 'LIST', section: 'Go', anchor: 'pingcap/tidb' }, relationship: 'references', bidirectional: true }, { source: { document: 'README', section: 'Web References/Architecture' }, target: { document: 'LIST', section: 'Go', anchor: 'k1LoW/ndiag' }, relationship: 'implements', bidirectional: true } ]; ``` **README Enhancement Pattern**: ```markdown ## Web References * Databases * TiDB: | *Related repos: [Go implementations](LIST.md#go) - [pingcap/tidb](LIST.md#go), [pingcap/tidb-tools](LIST.md#go)* ``` ### Component 5: Platform Migration Tracker **Purpose**: Systematically track repository migrations between hosting platforms. **Migration States**: ```typescript enum MigrationStatus { NONE = "none", // Single platform only MIRROR = "mirror", // Active on multiple platforms (mirror) MIGRATED = "migrated", // Moved from one to another ARCHIVED_MIGRATED = "archived_migrated" // Original archived, moved to new platform } interface PlatformInfo { platform: 'github' | 'codeberg' | 'gitlab' | 'gitea' | 'aws-codecommit'; url: string; status: 'active' | 'archived' | 'deprecated'; isPrimary: boolean; migrationDate?: string; notes?: string; } interface MigrationRecord { repoId: string; migrationStatus: MigrationStatus; platforms: PlatformInfo[]; migrationHistory: { date: string; from: string; to: string; reason?: string; }[]; } ``` **Detection Logic**: - Parse description for keywords: "Moved to", "Mirror", "convenience mirror", "Codeberg", "GitLab" - Check archive status + description mentions - Verify URL accessibility (if tooling permits) - Manual verification for uncertain cases ### Component 6: Content Update Manager **Purpose**: Identify stale content and suggest modern additions, especially for AI/ML and other rapidly evolving topics. **Update Strategy**: ```typescript interface UpdateCandidate { section: string; currentContent: Reference[]; issues: { type: 'stale' | 'missing' | 'deprecated' | 'incorrect'; severity: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; description: string; }[]; suggestions: { action: 'add' | 'update' | 'remove' | 'reorganize'; content: any; rationale: string; }[]; } // AI/ML modernization rules: const aimlTopics = { foundational: [ "Large Language Models (LLMs)", "Transformer Architecture", "Attention Mechanisms" ], infrastructure: [ "Vector Databases", "Embedding Models", "GPU Computing (AWS Neuron, CUDA)" ], techniques: [ "RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation)", "Fine-tuning (LoRA, QLoRA, PEFT)", "Prompt Engineering", "Context Management" ], tooling: [ "LangChain / LlamaIndex", "Model Evaluation Frameworks", "Inference Optimization", "Multi-Agent Systems" ] }; ``` **Starred Repository Mining for AI/ML**: ``` Based on current starred repos, extract: - mem0ai/mem0 (Apache 2.0, ⭐️44063) - Universal memory layer - awslabs/mcp (Apache 2.0, ⭐️7595) - AWS MCP Servers - deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 (MIT, ⭐️100652) - DeepSeek LLM - chroma-core/chroma (Apache 2.0, ⭐️24826) - Vector database - HKUDS/LightRAG (MIT, ⭐️25654) - RAG implementation - neuml/txtai (Apache 2.0, ⭐️11900) - AI framework - And more... Propose new "AI/ML" Web References structure with these as examples. ``` ## Data Models ### Repository Data Model ```yaml # repositories.yml (canonical source) schema_version: "1.0" last_updated: "2024-12-10T18:00:00Z" generated_by: "encodedfox/stargazer" total_count: 839 repositories: - id: "github-com-encodedfox-stargazer" # Core Identity name: "stargazer" owner: "encodedfox" full_name: "encodedfox/stargazer" # Multi-Platform Support platforms: - platform: "github" url: "https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer" status: "active" is_primary: true last_verified: "2024-12-10" # Metadata description: "Generate awesome list from your GitHub stars" primary_language: "Go" license: "MIT License" license_spdx: "MIT" stars: 150 topics: ["cli", "github", "golang", "awesome-list"] homepage: "" # Classification categories: - "development-tools" - "github-management" readme_sections: - "GitHub Projects" web_reference_topics: [] # Quality Tracking archive_status: false archive_date: null last_commit_date: "2024-11-15" last_star_update: "2024-12-10" quality_score: 85 # Source Tracking source: "github_stars" added_date: "2023-05-20" manually_curated: false curator_notes: "" ``` ### Manual Additions Data Model ```yaml # manual_additions.yml schema_version: "1.0" # Projects not in starred repos but manually added manual_projects: - id: "manual-nocobase" name: "NocoBase" description: "No-Code/Low-Code Development Platform" platforms: - platform: "github" url: "https://github.com/nocobase/nocobase" status: "active" is_primary: true metadata: primary_language: "TypeScript" license: "Apache License 2.0" classification: categories: ["low-code", "no-code", "platform"] readme_sections: ["GitHub Projects"] source: "manual" curator_notes: "Self-hosted alternative to Airtable/AppSmith" - id: "manual-matomo" name: "Matomo" description: "Web Analytics Tool" platforms: - platform: "github" url: "https://github.com/matomo-org/matomo" status: "active" # ... similar structure ``` ### Web References Data Model ```yaml # web_references.yml schema_version: "1.0" references: - id: "martinfowler-poeaa" title: "Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture (P of EAA)" url: "https://www.martinfowler.com/eaaCatalog/index.html" author: "Martin Fowler" category: "Architecture" subcategory: "Patterns" content_type: "catalog" difficulty: "intermediate" publication_date: "2002" last_verified: "2024-12-10" related_references: - "martinfowler-domain-model" - "martinfowler-anemic-domain" related_repos: - "bxcodec/go-clean-arch" - "gsvaldevieso/go-dream-architecture" tags: ["design-patterns", "enterprise", "architecture"] - id: "ai-ml-llm-intro" title: "Large Language Models Introduction" url: "TBD" # To be added category: "AI/ML" subcategory: "Foundation Models" content_type: "tutorial" difficulty: "introductory" last_verified: null related_repos: - "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3" - "mem0ai/mem0" tags: ["llm", "ai", "machine-learning"] status: "proposed" # proposed, active, deprecated ``` ### Books Data Model ```yaml # books.yml schema_version: "1.0" books: - id: "bass-designing-software-architectures" title: "Designing Software Architectures" subtitle: "A Practical Approach with ADD" authors: ["Len Bass", "Paul Clements", "Rick Kazman"] category: "Architecture" subcategory: "Design Methods" isbn: "978-0134390789" publication_year: 2016 edition: "1st" related_books: - id: "bass-software-architecture-practice" relationship: "companion" note: "Covers processes" - id: "clements-documenting-software-architectures" relationship: "companion" note: "Covers diagrams" expansion_topics: - "distributed-systems" - "microservices" - "data-architecture" related_web_references: - "martinfowler-poeaa" - "simon-wardley-mapping" related_repos: - "k1LoW/ndiag" - "blushft/go-diagrams" ``` ## Architecture Decisions ### Decision 1: Extend Stargazer Output vs. Replace It **Status**: ✅ Decided - Extend stargazer output **Context**: The repository uses [`encodedfox/stargazer`](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer) tool (updated fork) to generate LIST.md and TABLE.md from GitHub stars. **Decision**: Preserve stargazer as the primary data source and build augmentation layer on top. **Rationale**: - Stargazer (encodedfox fork) handles GitHub API authentication and rate limiting - Updated fork provides better maintenance and feature support - Existing generation logic works well for basic LIST/TABLE formats - Adding manual YAML files allows extension without forking stargazer - Merge strategy: `stargazer_output + manual_additions + enrichments = canonical_data` **Implementation**: ```bash # Workflow $ stargazer --output=raw_data.json $ enhance_data raw_data.json manual_additions.yml > canonical_data.json $ validate_data canonical_data.json > validation_report.json $ generate_docs canonical_data.json ``` ### Decision 2: Canonical Data Format **Status**: ✅ Decided - YAML for human-editable, JSON for machine processing **Options Considered**: 1. Single JSON file (machine-optimized) 2. Single YAML file (human-readable) 3. Hybrid: YAML source → JSON processing **Decision**: Use YAML for manual files and JSON for generated/processed data. **Rationale**: - YAML better for human editing of manual additions and configurations - JSON better for programmatic processing and validation - Conversion YAML→JSON is trivial - Allows version control of human-readable source while optimizing processing ### Decision 3: README Update Strategy **Status**: ✅ Decided - Injection markers with template sections **Options Considered**: 1. Full regeneration of README.md (loses manual content) 2. Manual updates only (inconsistent, error-prone) 3. Marked sections for auto-update with manual preservation **Decision**: Use HTML comment markers for auto-update regions while preserving manual content. **Implementation Example**: ```markdown ## Web References ### Related Repositories * Architecture Tools: [Go](LIST.md#go) - [k1LoW/ndiag](LIST.md#go), [blushft/go-diagrams](LIST.md#go) * Databases: [Go](LIST.md#go) - [pingcap/tidb](LIST.md#go), [cockroachdb/cockroach](LIST.md#go) * Architecture * MartinFowler's Patterns... ``` ### Decision 4: Platform Migration Representation **Status**: ✅ Decided - Inline notation with primary + alternative URLs **Options Considered**: 1. Separate section for migrated repos 2. Inline notation in description 3. Structured platform list per repo **Decision**: Use structured platform list in canonical data, inline notation in generated markdown. **Display Examples**: LIST.md: ```markdown - [technomancy/leiningen](https://github.com/technomancy/leiningen) - Moved to Codeberg; this is a convenience mirror [🔄→Codeberg](https://codeberg.org/leiningen/leiningen) (⭐️7301) ``` TABLE.md: ```markdown | [technomancy/leiningen](https://github.com/technomancy/leiningen) | Moved to Codeberg; mirror [🔄→Codeberg](https://codeberg.org/leiningen/leiningen) | - | ⭐️7301 | ``` ### Decision 5: README GitHub Projects Section Format **Status**: ✅ Decided - Structured list with complete metadata **Current Format** (incomplete): ```markdown ## GitHub Projects * NDiag - * NocoBase — — No-Code/Low-Code Development Platform ``` **Proposed Format**: ```markdown ## GitHub Projects Projects of special interest beyond the comprehensive starred lists below. ### Architecture & Documentation * [NDiag](https://github.com/k1LoW/ndiag) - High-level architecture diagramming/documentation tool [MIT] ⭐️189 📑[Listed in Go](LIST.md#go) * [GoReleaser](https://github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser) - Release engineering, simplified [MIT] ⭐️15333 📑[Listed in Go](LIST.md#go) ### Development Platforms * [NocoBase](https://github.com/nocobase/nocobase) - No-Code/Low-Code Development Platform [Apache 2.0] ⭐️TBD 🆕*Manual Addition* * [Gin](https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin) - High-performance HTTP web framework for Go [MIT] ⭐️87313 📑[Listed in Go](LIST.md#go) ### Self-Hosted Applications * [Matomo](https://github.com/matomo-org/matomo) - Web Analytics Tool [GPL-3.0] ⭐️TBD 🆕*Manual Addition* * [Nextcloud](https://github.com/nextcloud/server) - File Sharing and Collaboration Platform [AGPL-3.0] ⭐️TBD 🆕*Manual Addition* * [Bitwarden](https://github.com/bitwarden/server) - Password Management [GPL-3.0] ⭐️TBD 📑[Listed in Multiple](LIST.md#go) (see vaultwarden) * [Ghost](https://github.com/TryGhost/Ghost) - Content Management System [MIT] ⭐️TBD 🆕*Manual Addition* ### External Platforms * [Forgejo](https://codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo) - Codeberg-hosted Git forge [MIT] 🔄*Codeberg* ``` ## Error Handling ### Validation Errors ```typescript enum ValidationErrorCode { DUPLICATE_REPO = "E001", MISSING_LICENSE = "E002", INVALID_URL = "E003", BROKEN_CROSS_REF = "E004", PLATFORM_MISMATCH = "E005", MISSING_METADATA = "E006" } interface ValidationError { code: ValidationErrorCode; severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info'; message: string; location: { file: string; line?: number; section?: string; }; suggestion: string; autoFixable: boolean; } ``` **Error Handling Strategy**: 1. **Errors** (E001-E003): Block generation, require manual resolution 2. **Warnings** (E004-E005): Allow generation, flag in report 3. **Info** (E006): Log only, suggest improvements ### Generation Failures ```typescript interface GenerationFailure { phase: 'ingestion' | 'validation' | 'augmentation' | 'generation' | 'integration'; component: string; error: Error; recoverable: boolean; fallback?: () => void; } // Fallback strategy: const fallbacks = { stargazer_fail: () => { // Use last known good raw_data.json console.log("Using cached stargazer output"); }, validation_fail: () => { // Generate with warnings, produce detailed report console.log("Generating with validation warnings"); }, template_fail: () => { // Keep existing files, report failure console.log("Preserving existing output files"); } }; ``` ## Testing Strategy ### Unit Tests **Template Generation**: ```typescript describe('ListTemplate', () => { it('should format archived repository correctly', () => { const repo = { name: 'test-repo', url: 'https://github.com/test/repo', description: 'Test repository', license: 'MIT License', stars: 100, isArchived: true }; const result = listTemplate.formatEntry(repo); expect(result).toBe( '- [test/repo](https://github.com/test/repo) - Test repository \\[*MIT License*\\] (⭐️100) *Archived!*' ); }); it('should format platform migration correctly', () => { const repo = { // ... with multiple platforms }; expect(result).toContain('[🔄→Codeberg]'); }); }); ``` **Validation Engine**: ```typescript describe('ValidationEngine', () => { it('should detect duplicate repositories', () => { const data = { repositories: [ { id: 'repo1', platforms: [{ url: 'https://github.com/test/repo' }] }, { id: 'repo2', platforms: [{ url: 'https://github.com/test/repo' }] } ] }; const errors = validator.validate(data); expect(errors).toContainEqual( expect.objectContaining({ code: ValidationErrorCode.DUPLICATE_REPO }) ); }); it('should flag missing README cross-references', () => { // Test cross-reference validation }); }); ``` **Cross-Reference Manager**: ```typescript describe('CrossReferenceManager', () => { it('should generate bidirectional links', () => { const data = loadTestData(); const refs = crossRefManager.generate(data); // Verify README -> LIST references exist expect(refs.readmeToList['Databases/TiDB']).toContain('pingcap/tidb'); // Verify reverse references expect(refs.listToReadme['pingcap/tidb']).toContain('Databases'); }); }); ``` ### Integration Tests **End-to-End Generation**: ```typescript describe('E2E Generation', () => { it('should generate all documents from canonical data', async () => { const canonical = await loadCanonicalData(); const listMd = await generateList(canonical); const tableMd = await generateTable(canonical); const readmeUpdates = await generateReadmeUpdates(canonical); // Verify consistency expect(countReposInList(listMd)).toBe(canonical.repositories.length); expect(countReposInTable(tableMd)).toBe(canonical.repositories.length); // Verify all README projects exist const readmeProjects = extractReadmeProjects(readmeUpdates); for (const project of readmeProjects) { const exists = findInCanonical(canonical, project.name); expect(exists).toBeTruthy(); } }); it('should maintain consistent star counts across formats', async () => { const listStars = extractStarCounts('LIST.md'); const tableStars = extractStarCounts('TABLE.md'); expect(listStars).toEqual(tableStars); }); }); ``` **Validation Integration**: ```typescript describe('Validation Integration', () => { it('should produce validation report', async () => { const report = await runValidation(); expect(report.summary.errors).toBe(0); expect(report.metrics.repos_by_platform).toHaveProperty('github'); expect(report.metrics.archived_count).toBeGreaterThan(0); }); }); ``` ### Manual Test Scenarios 1. **Repository Migration Workflow**: - Start: Repo active on GitHub - Action: Repo archives and moves to Codeberg - Test: Update canonical data with migration - Verify: Both platforms shown in output with correct status 2. **Manual Project Addition**: - Start: New project identified (not starred) - Action: Add to manual_additions.yml - Test: Regenerate all documents - Verify: Project appears in README, validation passes 3. **Web Reference Update**: - Start: New AI/ML resources identified - Action: Add to web_references.yml with category - Test: Generate README with cross-references - Verify: Links to related starred repos appear 4. **Cross-Reference Navigation**: - Start: User reading Web References - Action: Click related repo link - Test: Navigate to LIST.md#language section - Verify: Repo appears, can navigate back to README ## Implementation Notes ### File Structure ``` omnidatum/ ├── .specly_dev/ │ └── specs/ │ └── 20251210-113942-reorganize-multi-format-repository-documentation-baseline/ │ ├── requirements.md │ ├── design.md │ └── tasks.md (future) ├── data/ │ ├── canonical/ │ │ ├── repositories.yml # Generated from stargazer + manual │ │ ├── manual_additions.yml # Human-edited │ │ ├── web_references.yml # Human-edited │ │ ├── books.yml # Human-edited │ │ └── metadata_index.json # Generated │ ├── templates/ │ │ ├── list.md.tmpl │ │ ├── table.md.tmpl │ │ ├── archive.md.tmpl │ │ └── readme_sections.md.tmpl │ └── cache/ │ ├── raw_stargazer.json # Stargazer output │ └── last_validation.json ├── docs/ │ └── ecosystems/ # Ecosystem detail files │ ├── bitwarden.md # Bitwarden implementations │ └── [others as needed] ├── scripts/ │ ├── fetch_stars.sh # Run stargazer (encodedfox version) │ ├── enhance_data.go # Merge and augment │ ├── validate.go # Run validations │ ├── generate_docs.go # Create LIST/TABLE/ARCHIVE │ ├── update_readme.go # Inject cross-references │ └── report.go # Generate reports ├── LIST.md # Generated (active repos only) ├── TABLE.md # Generated (active repos only) ├── ARCHIVE.md # Generated (archived/inactive repos) ├── ARCHIVE_TABLE.md # Generated (archived/inactive, table format) ├── README.md # Partially generated ├── .gitignore └── LICENSE ``` ### Technology Choices #### Processing Language Analysis **Context**: The repository contains 839 starred projects with significant representation across multiple systems languages. Local development environment has Rust, Zig, Koka, Nim, OCaml, and Go available. Current generation uses [`encodedfox/stargazer`](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer) (Go-based), which you maintain. **Strategy**: Enhance your existing stargazer repository with improved data format and algorithms, potentially migrating to a more performant or expressive language. **Language Evaluation Matrix**: | Criterion | Go (Current) | Rust | Zig | Nim | OCaml | Koka | |-----------|-------------|------|-----|-----|-------|------| | **Performance** | Fast | Fastest | Very Fast | Fast | Fast | Fast | | **Memory Safety** | GC, runtime checks | Memory safe, no GC | Manual, compile-time | GC | GC + inference | GC | | **Ecosystem** | Excellent | Excellent | Growing | Moderate | Mature | Limited | | **YAML/JSON Libraries** | Excellent | Excellent | Good | Good | Excellent | Limited | | **GitHub API Clients** | Excellent | Good | Limited | Moderate | Good | Limited | | **Template Systems** | Built-in | Multiple (tera, handlebars) | Custom | Multiple | Multiple | Limited | | **Learning Curve** | Low | High | Medium | Low-Medium | Medium | High | | **Build Speed** | Fast | Slower | Very Fast | Fast | Very Fast | Fast | | **Cross-compilation** | Excellent | Excellent | Excellent | Good | Good | Limited | | **Community Size** | Very Large | Large | Growing | Small | Medium | Very Small | | **Maintenance Burden** | Low | Low | Medium | Medium | Low-Medium | High | | **Type System** | Structural | Algebraic | C-like | Flexible | Algebraic + inference | Effect types | | **Pattern Matching** | Limited | Excellent | Manual | Good | Excellent | Excellent | **Detailed Analysis**: **Rust** ⭐ **Top Recommendation**: - ✅ **Pros**: Memory safety without GC, excellent performance, strong type system, great for CLI tools, comprehensive error handling - ✅ **Libraries**: `serde` (serialization), `serde_yaml`, `serde_json`, `octocrab` (GitHub API), `tera`/`askama` (templates) - ✅ **Ecosystem**: Growing significantly, many modern tools written in Rust (see starred: `bat`, `fd`, `ripgrep`, `tokei`, `starship`, `uv`) - ✅ **Pattern Matching**: Algebraic data types perfect for modeling repository states and validations - ⚠️ **Cons**: Steeper learning curve, slower compilation times (mitigated by incremental builds) - 🎯 **Use Case Fit**: **Excellent** - ideal for data processing, CLI tools, template generation **OCaml** ⭐ **Strong Alternative**: - ✅ **Pros**: Mature type system with inference, excellent for data transformation, fast compilation, powerful pattern matching - ✅ **Libraries**: `yojson` (JSON), `yaml` (YAML), `cohttp`/`piaf` (HTTP), `jingoo` (Jinja2-like templates) - ✅ **Ecosystem**: Mature and stable, strong in data processing and compiler tools (see starred: `semgrep`, `dune`, `opam`) - ✅ **Type Inference**: Write less code while maintaining type safety - ✅ **Functional**: Natural fit for data transformation pipelines - ⚠️ **Cons**: Smaller community than Go/Rust, less common in CLI tool space - 🎯 **Use Case Fit**: **Excellent** - OCaml excels at structured data manipulation and code generation **Zig**: - ✅ **Pros**: No hidden control flow, excellent C interop, very fast builds, explicit error handling, comptime - ✅ **Libraries**: Growing ecosystem, direct C library usage via `@cImport` - ⚠️ **Cons**: Pre-1.0, fewer high-level libraries, YAML/JSON libraries less mature - ⚠️ **Ecosystem**: Younger, but see starred interest: `ziglang/zig`, `oven-sh/bun`, `tigerbeetle/tigerbeetle` - 🎯 **Use Case Fit**: **Good** - capable but requires more low-level work **Nim**: - ✅ **Pros**: Python-like syntax, compiles to C, good performance, readable code, macro system - ✅ **Libraries**: `yaml`, `json`, `parsetoml`, `karax` (templates), `nim-markdown` - ⚠️ **Cons**: Smaller community, fewer GitHub API clients, less tooling maturity - 🎯 **Use Case Fit**: **Acceptable** - readable but fewer established patterns **Go (Current)**: - ✅ **Pros**: Simple, fast compilation, excellent stdlib, great for CLI tools, trivial cross-compilation - ✅ **Libraries**: `gopkg.in/yaml.v3`, `encoding/json`, `google/go-github`, `text/template` - ✅ **Current State**: Your stargazer implementation already works, proven solution - ⚠️ **Cons**: Verbose error handling, no algebraic data types, limited pattern matching - 🎯 **Use Case Fit**: **Excellent** - already proven for this exact use case **Koka**: - ✅ **Pros**: Effect system, functional programming, mathematical correctness, innovative type system - ⚠️ **Cons**: Academic language, minimal ecosystem, no mature GitHub API clients, limited practical libraries - ❌ **Use Case Fit**: **Poor** - designed for research/correctness proofs, not practical data processing --- **Top 3 Recommendations**: 1. **Rust** (Best Overall) - Performance + Safety + Ecosystem + Modern Tooling 2. **OCaml** (Best for Data Processing) - Type System + Functional + Fast Compilation + Mature 3. **Go** (Safest) - Current working solution, quickest path to completion **Implementation Strategy** (No Fork Required): Since you own encodedfox/stargazer, the enhancement strategy is streamlined: ```bash # Enhancement Strategy 1. Create feature branch in encodedfox/stargazer: feature/enhanced-output 2. Extend current data model to include enhanced metadata 3. Add new output formats (detailed JSON, validation mode) 4. Implement incremental changes: - Phase 1: Enhanced JSON output format (backward compatible) - Phase 2: Add platform tracking and migration detection - Phase 3: Add validation engine - Phase 4: Add template improvements - Phase 5: [If migrating language] Implement parallel version in new language 5. Maintain backward compatibility with existing CLI flags 6. Add new features via optional flags (--enhanced-json, --validate, --platforms) # If migrating to new language: 1. Create new branch: rewrite/[rust|ocaml|zig] 2. Port incrementally with interop testing 3. Maintain feature parity before switching default 4. Release both versions initially (stargazer-go, stargazer-rs/ml) 5. Deprecate old version after community validation ``` **Alternative: Zig** (if control and simplicity are priorities) - Simpler than Rust, faster builds than Go - Good C interop for leveraging existing libraries - Explicit resource management aligns with systems programming background - Growing ecosystem shows in your starred repos **Rejected: Koka** - Insufficient ecosystem for practical tooling tasks **Decision Point Required**: - **Option A**: Rewrite in Rust (recommended, best long-term value) - **Option B**: Enhance Go version (quickest path, proven technology) - **Option C**: Hybrid approach (Go for now, Rust exploration in parallel) **Template Engine Options by Language**: **If Rust**: - [`tera`](https://github.com/Keats/tera) - Jinja2-inspired, powerful - [`handlebars-rust`](https://github.com/sunng87/handlebars-rust) - Handlebars syntax - [`askama`](https://github.com/djc/askama) - Compile-time templates (type-safe) - **Recommendation**: `tera` for flexibility or `askama` for type safety **If OCaml**: - [`jingoo`](https://github.com/tategakibunko/jingoo) - Jinja2 templates for OCaml - [`tyxml`](https://github.com/ocsigen/tyxml) - Type-safe HTML/XML (adaptable for markdown) - [`mustache.ml`](https://github.com/rgrinberg/ocaml-mustache) - Mustache templates - **Recommendation**: `jingoo` for familiar syntax, `tyxml` for type safety **If Go** (current): - [`text/template`](https://pkg.go.dev/text/template) - Built-in, sufficient - [`pongo2`](https://github.com/flosch/pongo2) - Django-inspired - **Current Choice**: `text/template` (no dependencies) **If Zig**: - Custom implementation or C library bindings - [`mustache-zig`](https://github.com/batiati/mustache-zig) - Mustache templates - **Recommendation**: Custom for simplicity **If Nim**: - [`nim-mustache`](https://github.com/soasme/nim-mustache) - Mustache templates - [`nim-templates`](https://nim-lang.org/docs/filters.html) - Built-in template filter - **Recommendation**: Built-in for simplicity **Validation**: JSON Schema + Custom Rules - **Rationale**: Standard validation for structure, custom for business logic - **Library**: [`github.com/xeipuuv/gojsonschema`](https://github.com/xeipuuv/gojsonschema) **Cross-Reference Detection**: Graph-based analysis - **Rationale**: Relationships form a graph (repos↔sections↔references) - **Library**: [`github.com/dominikbraun/graph`](https://github.com/dominikbraun/graph) or custom implementation ### Migration from Current to Proposed State **Phase 1: Data Extraction** 1. Parse current LIST.md and TABLE.md into structured format 2. Extract README projects into manual_additions.yml template 3. Extract Web References into web_references.yml 4. Extract Books into books.yml **Phase 2: Validation & Gap Analysis** 1. Run validation against extracted data 2. Identify inconsistencies between LIST and TABLE 3. Find README projects not in starred lists 4. Detect missing cross-references 5. Generate gap analysis report **Phase 3: Manual Curation** 1. Review validation report 2. Research missing GitHub Projects URLs 3. Identify AI/ML update candidates 4. Propose Web References restructuring 5. Document findings in manual_additions.yml and web_references.yml **Phase 4: Generation & Verification** 1. Merge all data sources into canonical format 2. Generate new LIST.md and TABLE.md 3. Update README.md with cross-references 4. Run final validation 5. Compare outputs with originals (diff) **Phase 5: Documentation & Handoff** 1. Document update procedures 2. Create runbook for future updates 3. Generate baseline summary for guidance requests 4. Version control all changes ### Backward Compatibility **Preserved Elements**: - ✅ LIST.md format and structure - ✅ TABLE.md format and structure - ✅ README.md manual sections remain editable - ✅ All existing URLs remain functional - ✅ Section anchors preserved for external links **Breaking Changes**: None anticipated **Migration Support**: - Old format validation (detect if current files are valid) - Incremental adoption (can run alongside existing process) - Rollback capability (version control + backup) ## Performance Considerations ### Processing Performance **Expected Processing Times** (839 repositories): - Data ingestion (stargazer): ~30 seconds (GitHub API limited) - Validation: <5 seconds - Template generation: <2 seconds - README updates: <1 second - **Total**: ~40 seconds for complete regeneration **Optimization Strategies**: - Cache GitHub API responses (24-hour TTL) - Parallel validation rule execution - Incremental updates (only changed repos) - Lazy cross-reference computation ### File Size Projections **Current Sizes**: - LIST.md: ~117 KB (977 lines) - TABLE.md: ~178 KB (1,149 lines) - README.md: ~6 KB (121 lines) **Projected Sizes After Enhancements**: - LIST.md: ~125 KB (+7% for platform notations) - TABLE.md: ~190 KB (+7% for platform columns) - README.md: ~12 KB (+100% for cross-references, expanded sections) - Data files: ~500 KB (canonical data in JSON/YAML) - Total: ~830 KB (manageable for git repository) ### Decision 6: Archive List Management **Status**: ✅ Decided - Separate archive list with searchable access **Decision**: Archived or inactive repositories (<10 stars, >2 years inactive) will be moved to separate `ARCHIVE.md` and `ARCHIVE_TABLE.md` files, organized by language category, with tooling support for searching across all lists. **Rationale**: - Keeps main lists (LIST.md, TABLE.md) focused on active, maintained projects - Preserves historical context and research value in dedicated archive files - Allows users to explicitly opt-in to viewing archived content via link or search flag - Reduces cognitive load when browsing primary lists **Implementation**: - Create `ARCHIVE.md` and `ARCHIVE_TABLE.md` with same format as main lists - Add archive status flag in canonical data: `archive_threshold: <10 stars AND >2 years inactive` - Generation tool creates both active and archive lists simultaneously - README links to archive: "See also: [Archived Projects](ARCHIVE.md) for historical references" - Search tooling flag: `--include-archived` for comprehensive search - Archive files maintain same language-based organization for consistency ### Decision 7: Handling Duplicate Implementations **Status**: ✅ Decided - Main repo with linked ecosystem detail file **Decision**: For projects with multiple implementations (e.g., Bitwarden ecosystem), the README references the main/official implementation with a link to a dedicated detail file listing all related projects in that ecosystem. **Example Structure**: README.md: ```markdown ### Self-Hosted Applications * [Bitwarden](https://github.com/bitwarden/server) - Official password management server [GPL-3.0] ⭐️TBD 📦 [See all Bitwarden implementations](docs/ecosystems/bitwarden.md) ``` docs/ecosystems/bitwarden.md: ```markdown # Bitwarden Ecosystem ## Official Implementation * [bitwarden/server](https://github.com/bitwarden/server) - Official Bitwarden server [GPL-3.0] ⭐️TBD ## Alternative Implementations * [dani-garcia/vaultwarden](../../LIST.md#rust) - Unofficial Rust implementation, lightweight [AGPL-3.0] ⭐️51763 * [VictorNine/bitwarden-go](../../LIST.md#go) - Golang implementation [MIT] ⭐️244 ## Related Tools * [gopasspw/gopass](../../LIST.md#go) - Compatible password manager [MIT] ⭐️6599 ``` **Rationale**: - Provides comprehensive view of ecosystem without cluttering README - Main README stays concise and focused on official/primary implementations - Detail files can be expanded as needed with comparisons, migration guides - Links back to starred lists (LIST.md) maintain single source of truth - Users searching for alternatives get complete picture ### Decision 8: README Inclusion Criteria **Status**: ✅ Decided - Star threshold + significance criteria **Decision**: Projects are included in README.md "GitHub Projects" section (beyond comprehensive starred lists) based on: 1. **Star Threshold**: >2000 stars on primary platform, OR 2. **Architectural Significance**: Represents important pattern or approach, OR 3. **Manual Curation**: Strategically selected for specific relevance (explicitly tracked projects including Codeberg) **Clarification**: All projects appear in starred lists (LIST.md/TABLE.md) by virtue of being GitHub-starred or explicitly tracked. README inclusion is a subset highlighting particularly significant projects. **Rationale**: - Clear quantitative threshold (2000 stars) indicates substantial community validation - Qualitative criteria (architectural significance) allows expert curation - Manual additions support strategic knowledge management and platform diversity goals - All repositories remain discoverable in comprehensive LIST/TABLE regardless of README inclusion **Implementation**: ```yaml # In canonical data: classification: readme_inclusion: true readme_inclusion_reason: "manual_curation" # or "star_threshold" or "architectural_significance" readme_section: "Development Platforms" significance_notes: "Strategic self-hosted alternative to commercial platforms" ``` **Validation Rule**: - Warn if README projects don't meet criteria (for periodic review) - Require `readme_inclusion_reason` field for audit trail ### Decision 9: Platform Statistics Placement **Status**: ✅ Decided - Flexible footer positioning with customizable format **Decision**: Platform count breakdown will be included in document footer by default, with flexible format that can be adjusted while maintaining consistent source data. **Default Footer Format**: ```markdown --- **[⬆ back to top](#contents)** ## Repository Statistics **Total Repositories**: 839 **By Platform**: - GitHub: 835 repositories (99.5%) - Codeberg: 4 repositories (0.5%) **By Status**: - Active: 797 repositories (95%) - Archived: 42 repositories (5%) **Last Updated**: 2024-12-10T18:00:00Z *Generated by [encodedfox/stargazer](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer)* ``` **Alternative Compact Format** (if preferred): ```markdown --- **Statistics**: 839 repos | GitHub: 835 | Codeberg: 4 | Active: 797 | Archived: 42 | Updated: 2024-12-10 *Generated by [stargazer](https://github.com/encodedfox/stargazer)* ``` **Rationale**: - Footer placement doesn't interfere with content flow or TOC - Provides transparency about data sources, freshness, and composition - Format flexibility accommodates user preference without re-engineering - Percentages help understand platform distribution at a glance - Easy to update programmatically from canonical data **Implementation**: Template-driven with configuration for format selection ### Decision 10: Multi-Language Classification **Status**: ✅ Decided - Primary language with optional percentage enrichment **Decision**: Accept stargazer's GitHub primary language classification as default categorization, while enriching canonical data with language percentage breakdown when available from GitHub API. Repositories with significant secondary languages (>30% of codebase) can be annotated. **Data Structure**: ```yaml repositories: - id: "example-multi-lang-repo" metadata: primary_language: "Go" language_breakdown: # Optional, from GitHub API if available Go: 65 Python: 25 Shell: 10 secondary_languages: ["Python"] # Languages >30% classification: language_category: "Go" # For LIST/TABLE categorization language_notes: "Significant Python components (25%)" # Optional annotation ``` **Display Options** (configurable): Minimal (default): ```markdown ## Go - [example/repo](https://github.com/example/repo) - Multi-language tool [MIT] (⭐️1000) ``` Enriched (if language_breakdown available): ```markdown ## Go - [example/repo](https://github.com/example/repo) - Multi-language tool *+Python(25%)* [MIT] (⭐️1000) ``` **Rationale**: - Maintains compatibility with stargazer's categorization scheme - Provides richer metadata for future search and filtering capabilities - Optional display notation doesn't clutter existing format - GitHub API `languages` endpoint provides percentage data: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages` - Supports discovery of multi-language projects without duplicate categorization **Research Action**: - Investigate GitHub API rate limits for language breakdown queries (may require caching strategy) - Determine if encodedfox/stargazer already captures this data (check output format) - If not available, treat as optional enhancement for future iteration ## Future Enhancements Beyond the baseline reorganization, potential improvements include: 1. **Automated Update Pipeline** - Scheduled GitHub Actions workflow for star count updates - Automated PR creation for detected changes - Dead link detection and reporting 2. **Enhanced Search** - Full-text search across all documents - Tag-based filtering - Topic clustering and recommendation 3. **Visualization** - Technology landscape map - Repository relationship graph - Trend analysis over time 4. **Integration** - Export to knowledge management systems - API for programmatic access - RSS feed for updates 5. **Quality Improvements** - Automated description enhancement using LLMs - Duplicate detection using semantic similarity - Category suggestion based on content analysis These enhancements are explicitly out of scope for the baseline reorganization but inform the design to ensure extensibility.

# Requirements Document
## Introduction
This requirements document defines the reorganization of the OmniDatum repository documentation to establish a consistent baseline for multi-format knowledge management. The repository currently maintains 839 GitHub starred repositories in two formats ([`LIST.md`](../../LIST.md) and [`TABLE.md`](../../TABLE.md)), along with supplementary documentation in [`README.md`](../../README.md) covering books, service concepts, web references, and additional project listings.
The primary challenges include: - Content synchronization across multiple document formats - Outdated sections requiring updates (especially AI/ML content from 2 years ago) - Inconsistent organization between sections - Missing cross-references between starred repos and README project listings - Need to track multiple hosting platforms (GitHub, Codeberg, GitLab, AWS CodeCommit) - Establishing a structured baseline for requesting unbiased guidance based on curated research
This reorganization will create a maintainable foundation that accurately reflects the current state of the knowledge base and supports future growth across multiple data sources (GitHub stars, selfh.st, awesome-selfhosted.net, and manual curation).
## Requirements
### Requirement 1: Content Synchronization
**User Story:** As a repository maintainer, I want all documents to reflect consistent and accurate information across formats, so that readers can trust any view of the data and avoid confusion from outdated or conflicting content.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN a GitHub repository appears in [`LIST.md`](../../LIST.md) THEN it SHALL also appear in [`TABLE.md`](../../TABLE.md) with identical name, URL, description, license, and star count 2. WHEN a repository is listed in the [`README.md`](../../README.md) "GitHub Projects" section THEN the system SHALL verify if it exists in [`LIST.md`](../../LIST.md) and [`TABLE.md`](../../TABLE.md) 3. WHEN a repository in "GitHub Projects" is not found in the starred lists THEN the system SHALL flag it for manual review or addition 4. IF a repository exists in the starred lists but not in "GitHub Projects" AND it matches thematic areas (Architecture, Databases, Security, AI/ML, Workflow, DevOps) THEN the system SHALL suggest adding it to appropriate README sections 5. WHEN synchronization is complete THEN the system SHALL generate a report showing all additions, conflicts, and missing cross-references
### Requirement 2: README Section Reorganization
**User Story:** As a knowledge seeker, I want the [`README.md`](../../README.md) sections to be logically organized and non-redundant, so that I can quickly find relevant information without encountering duplicate or misplaced content.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN reviewing the "Books" section THEN the system SHALL identify potential expansion topics based on referenced content in Web References and starred repositories 2. WHEN reviewing "Service Concepts & Examples" THEN the system SHALL identify overlapping content with "Web References" AND create a consolidated structure proposal 3. WHEN examining "Web References" sub-sections THEN the system SHALL evaluate ordering logic AND suggest improvements based on: - Logical dependency order (foundational concepts before advanced topics) - Thematic clustering (group related technologies) - Frequency of reference in starred repositories 4. IF content appears in multiple sections THEN the system SHALL propose either cross-referencing OR moving to a single canonical location 5. WHEN reorganization is proposed THEN the system SHALL maintain all existing URLs and references to prevent broken links
### Requirement 3: Multi-Platform Tracking
**User Story:** As a repository maintainer, I want to track projects across multiple hosting platforms (GitHub, Codeberg, GitLab, AWS CodeCommit), so that I can maintain a platform-agnostic knowledge base that survives platform migrations.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN a repository is archived on GitHub AND has migrated to Codeberg THEN the document SHALL include both the archived GitHub link and active Codeberg link with migration notation 2. WHEN adding new repositories THEN the system SHALL support platform identifiers for GitHub, Codeberg, GitLab, Gitea, and AWS CodeCommit 3. IF a repository description mentions "Moved to Codeberg" OR "Mirror" THEN the system SHALL parse and extract the canonical location 4. WHEN displaying platform information THEN the system SHALL use consistent iconography or notation (e.g., [GitHub], [Codeberg], [GitLab]) 5. WHEN generating statistics THEN the system SHALL report repository counts per platform AND archive status
### Requirement 4: Content Update and Modernization
**User Story:** As a technology researcher, I want documentation sections to reflect current state-of-the-art (especially AI/ML content), so that I receive relevant and timely guidance based on modern practices.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN reviewing the "AI/ML" sub-section in Web References THEN the system SHALL identify content over 18 months old AND flag for review 2. IF AI/ML section lacks recent topics (LLMs, Vector Databases, RAG, Fine-tuning, Agents, MCP) THEN the system SHALL propose additions based on starred repositories 3. WHEN scanning starred repositories for AI/ML tools THEN the system SHALL extract and categorize them by function: - Large Language Models and frameworks - Vector databases and search - RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) systems - ML operations and orchestration - Training and inference platforms 4. WHEN updating Web References THEN the system SHALL preserve historical links while adding new ones with date annotations 5. IF a starred repository with significant stars (>1000) relates to AI/ML AND is not in Web References THEN the system SHALL propose adding it
### Requirement 5: GitHub Projects Section Enhancement
**User Story:** As a repository maintainer, I want the GitHub Projects section to clearly distinguish between starred repos and additional manual entries, so that I understand the origin and selection criteria for each project.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN a project is listed in "GitHub Projects" THEN it SHALL include either a GitHub URL OR a description explaining why no URL exists 2. IF a GitHub Projects entry exists in starred lists THEN it SHALL include a cross-reference notation 3. WHEN projects lack URLs (NocoBase, Matomo, Nextcloud, Bitwarden, Ghost) THEN the system SHALL research and propose complete entries with: - Official repository URLs - Brief description - License information - Primary language 4. WHEN adding projects to this section THEN the system SHALL require justification tags (e.g., [Self-Hosted], [Architecture Tool], [Security], [Data Management]) 5. IF a project in this section is archived OR deprecated THEN it SHALL be annotated with status and suggested alternatives
### Requirement 6: Service Concepts Documentation Enhancement
**User Story:** As an architect or developer, I want the Service Concepts & Examples section to provide complete, actionable references with working examples, so that I can evaluate patterns for potential implementation.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN documenting a service concept THEN it SHALL include: - Concept name and brief description - Link to official documentation (if available) - Link to repository (noting archive status) - At least one alternative or successor (if archived) - Related technologies or patterns 2. IF a service concept is archived (Heroic, Akutan) THEN the document SHALL suggest modern alternatives from starred repositories 3. WHEN identifying service patterns in starred repositories THEN the system SHALL propose new Service Concepts entries for patterns with: - Multiple implementations (>2 repos) - High community interest (>5000 stars combined) - Architectural significance 4. WHEN Web References overlap with Service Concepts THEN the system SHALL create bidirectional links 5. IF a service concept lacks examples THEN the system SHALL search starred repositories for implementations
### Requirement 7: Web References Section Restructuring
**User Story:** As a researcher, I want the Web References section organized in a hierarchical, dependency-aware structure with current content, so that I can learn topics progressively and access relevant modern resources.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN evaluating current sub-sections (Architecture, DataMesh, Microservices, Databases, Security, AI/ML, Crypto, Languages) THEN the system SHALL propose optimal ordering based on: - Conceptual dependencies (foundational first) - Learning path progression - Cross-reference frequency 2. WHEN sub-sections contain multiple items THEN they SHALL be ordered by: - Introductory/overview content first - Specific implementations second - Advanced or specialized topics last 3. IF a sub-section has >15 items THEN the system SHALL propose tertiary groupings 4. WHEN AI/ML section is updated THEN it SHALL include categories for: - Foundation Models and LLMs - Vector Databases - RAG and Context Management - Model Evaluation and Benchmarking - Training and Fine-tuning - Agents and Orchestration 5. WHEN restructuring is complete THEN the document SHALL include a "Last Updated" timestamp per section
### Requirement 8: Books Section Expansion
**User Story:** As a learner, I want the Books section to comprehensively cover referenced topics throughout the repository, so that I can pursue deeper study of foundational concepts.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN analyzing Web References and starred repositories THEN the system SHALL identify major topics (Data Engineering, Distributed Systems, Functional Programming, Security, DevOps) lacking book recommendations 2. IF a topic appears in Web References with >5 links OR has >20 starred repositories THEN it SHALL be evaluated for book recommendation addition 3. WHEN proposing book additions THEN entries SHALL include: - Complete title and author - Brief relevance statement (how it relates to repository themes) - ISBN or reliable source link 4. WHEN organizing books THEN they SHALL be grouped by major domain area (Architecture, Data, Systems, Security, Development Practices) 5. IF existing books have unclear relationships THEN the structure SHALL show parent-child or prerequisite relationships
### Requirement 9: Baseline Documentation for Guidance Requests
**User Story:** As a decision-maker, I want a structured baseline that represents my research and views, so that I can request guidance from external sources (AI, advisors, communities) with proper context and receive unbiased recommendations.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN creating a baseline summary document THEN it SHALL include: - Repository scope and purpose statement - Content inventory (counts by category, platform, language) - Thematic focus areas with priority indicators - Known gaps or areas for exploration - Criteria for inclusion (star thresholds, topic relevance, quality indicators) 2. WHEN documenting research sources THEN it SHALL list: - Primary sources (GitHub stars, selfh.st, awesome-selfhosted.net) - Collection methodology - Last update timestamps - Known biases or limitations 3. IF requesting external guidance THEN the baseline SHALL provide: - Clear context about current state - Specific questions or areas for input - Constraints or preferences - Expected output format 4. WHEN the baseline is established THEN it SHALL be version-controlled with change tracking 5. WHEN guidance is received THEN responses SHALL be documented with source attribution and integration decisions
### Requirement 10: Format-Specific Maintenance Requirements
**User Story:** As a repository maintainer, I want clear specifications for each document format, so that automated updates maintain consistency and each format serves its intended purpose effectively.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN updating [`LIST.md`](../../LIST.md) THEN it SHALL maintain: - Bullet list format with GitHub links - License in brackets format: `\[*License Name*\]` - Star count in parentheses: `(⭐️count)` - Archive status notation: `*Archived!*` suffix - Language-based sections with alphabetical ordering within sections 2. WHEN updating [`TABLE.md`](../../TABLE.md) THEN it SHALL maintain: - Markdown table format with columns: Name, Description, License, Stars - Archive status in row: `(*archived*)` after description - "back to top" navigation links between sections - Consistent column alignment 3. WHEN both formats are updated THEN they SHALL be generated from the same data source to ensure consistency 4. WHEN [`README.md`](../../README.md) is updated THEN it SHALL maintain: - Hierarchical bullet structure for Web References - Consistent link format for all URLs - Section-specific formatting conventions 5. WHEN any document is modified THEN validation SHALL check: - All URLs are accessible or marked as archived - No duplicate entries within same section - Cross-references are bidirectional where appropriate
### Requirement 11: Discovery and Navigation Enhancement
**User Story:** As a user exploring the repository, I want intuitive navigation and discovery mechanisms, so that I can find related content across different sections and formats without manual searching.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN viewing any document THEN it SHALL include a table of contents with anchor links to all major sections 2. IF content relates to multiple categories THEN it SHALL include cross-reference links using format: "See also: [Section Name](path#anchor)" 3. WHEN a starred repository relates to a Web Reference topic THEN the Web Reference SHALL link to the relevant language section in LIST.md or TABLE.md 4. IF a README section discusses a concept AND relevant starred repos exist THEN it SHALL include "Related repositories: [Language](LIST.md#language)" references 5. WHEN navigation structure is complete THEN the system SHALL support both top-down (README → detailed lists) and bottom-up (starred repo → concept documentation) discovery
### Requirement 12: Quality and Metadata Tracking
**User Story:** As a repository maintainer, I want to track quality indicators and metadata for all content, so that I can prioritize updates and identify high-value versus low-value entries.
#### Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN repositories are tracked THEN the system SHALL maintain metadata: - Last star count update timestamp - Archive status and date detected - Migration status (if moved to different platform) - Last verification date for URL accessibility - Quality score based on stars, activity, and relevance 2. IF a repository has <10 stars AND has been archived THEN it SHALL be flagged for potential removal 3. WHEN Web References are maintained THEN each link SHALL track: - Last verified date - Content type (article, documentation, book, video) - Difficulty level (introductory, intermediate, advanced) 4. WHEN Books are listed THEN they SHALL include: - Publication year - Edition information (if applicable) - Relationship to other books (prerequisite, companion, successor) 5. WHEN quality tracking is implemented THEN reports SHALL identify: - Stale content (>2 years since verification) - High-value additions (trending repos matching themes) - Low-engagement content (candidates for archival)
## Success Criteria
The reorganization will be considered successful when:
1. **Consistency**: All three main documents (LIST.md, TABLE.md, README.md) reference the same set of repositories with synchronized metadata 2. **Completeness**: All projects mentioned in README.md either exist in starred lists OR are documented as manual additions with justification 3. **Currency**: AI/ML and other technology sections reflect state-of-the-art as of 2024-2025 4. **Navigability**: Users can traverse between formats and sections using clear cross-references 5. **Maintainability**: Update processes are documented and can be executed with minimal manual intervention 6. **Platform Awareness**: All hosting platforms are tracked with migration paths documented 7. **Baseline Established**: A summary document exists that can contextualize requests for external guidance
## Out of Scope
- Automated synchronization tooling (beyond identifying requirements for it) - Real-time star count updates from GitHub API - Addition of new starred repositories (focus is on organizing existing 839) - Creation of new web reference content (focus is on organizing existing) - Development of custom visualization or browsing interface - Translation to other human languages

# Implementation Tasks
## Overview
This task list covers the implementation of the OmniDatum repository documentation reorganization, following the approved requirements and design. The implementation uses Rust for enhanced processing capabilities while maintaining backward compatibility with existing output formats.
**Implementation Approach**: Incremental development with validation at each stage, building toward a complete Rust-based enhancement of the stargazer tool with new capabilities for multi-platform tracking, validation, and cross-referencing.
## Tasks
- [x] 1.
